content: pydantic-settings 2.14.2 provides BaseSettings class that reads configuration from environment variables and .env files with Pydantic v2 validation. class GitHubConfig(BaseSettings): github_pat: str = ''; github_org: str = ''. Fields map to env vars by uppercasing the field name. model_config = SettingsConfigDict(env_prefix='') or a custom prefix. Missing required env vars raise ValidationError at instantiation time, enabling fail-fast startup config validation. Alternative: the existing project pattern uses os.getenv() directly (see persistence/database.py PECP_DATABASE_URL). For the PECP GitHub integration, pydantic-settings is the better choice because: (1) it validates that GITHUB_PAT and GITHUB_ORG are present strings (not None) at startup, preventing silent misconfiguration; (2) it fits the existing Pydantic v2 stack. The python-dotenv package already in pyproject.toml provides .env loading for local development — pydantic-settings can read from .env files directly when python-dotenv is installed.
